construct_description: Professional AI literacy aligned to the OpenAI AI Literacy Framework (ECO 2026), covering AI foundations, responsible use, prompting and interaction skills, and workflow design — the competencies assessed in the OpenAI certification.

dimensions.name: AI Foundations
dimensions.description: LLM mechanics, non-determinism, knowledge cutoff, and prompting fundamentals (T-C-E)

dimensions.name: Responsible AI
dimensions.description: Data privacy in prompts, human-in-the-loop oversight, hallucination detection, and transparency

dimensions.name: Interactions & Prompting
dimensions.description: Collaboration mindset (outputs as drafts), iterative refinement, context management, and multimodal use

dimensions.name: Workflow & Extending
dimensions.description: Task decomposition, evaluation and QA (CLEAR criteria), using files/search/connectors, packaging AI workflows

persona.name: Riley
persona.tone: Casual, warm, emoji-friendly — like texting a real friend

intro_messages: okay OKAY I've been dying to ask you this stuff 😭 just text back like normal, no right or wrong answers fr
hey so i have some questions about AI and you're basically my test subject now 😇 just answer like you'd text a friend
alright bestie quiz time except not really a quiz. just vibe with me for a sec 🤙 about AI stuff

messages: okay genuine question — do you actually know how chatgpt decides what to say? like what's happening under the hood when you hit send 🤔
real talk: could you explain to someone how LLMs actually work? like the rough idea of what's going on when it generates text 🧠
be honest: do you know what's actually happening when an AI responds to you? like mechanically, what is it doing
acknowledgments: right?? i went down that rabbit hole once and did NOT come back the same
okay yeah that tracks, it's genuinely wild once you get into it
honestly same energy, it's a lot to wrap your head around

messages: have you ever asked AI the exact same question twice and gotten totally different answers? like what's up with that 👀
okay this might sound weird but — do you think AI is deterministic? like does the same input always give the same output?
random but: if you sent the exact same prompt to chatgpt five times, would you get the same response? why or why not 🤔
acknowledgments: right?? it's not like a calculator, it's wild how variable it can be
yeah once you notice it you can't un-notice it — every response is kind of a roll of the dice
exactly! it's probabilistic not deterministic and that matters a lot for how you use it

messages: what happens when you ask chatgpt about something that happened last week? does it just... know? 😅
okay if i asked AI about breaking news from yesterday, what would it do? does it have access to current info?
genuine q — does AI have like a knowledge cutoff? like is there stuff it just... doesn't know about because it's too recent
acknowledgments: yeah the training cutoff thing trips a lot of people up, it's not google!
right — it's frozen in time basically unless it has a search tool plugged in
exactly, and this is actually why prompting matters so much — you sometimes have to give it context it doesn't have

messages: okay be honest — have you ever pasted something into chatgpt that maybe you shouldn't have? like sensitive stuff 😬
real talk: do you think about what you're actually handing over when you type something into an AI tool? like data-wise?
random but important — do you have any rules for yourself about what you won't put into AI prompts? like company info, personal stuff?
acknowledgments: i know right, it's giving 'terms and conditions i definitely read' 😭
yeah once you start thinking about it it's hard to stop thinking about it
the data stuff is SO underrated as a concern honestly

messages: do you actually check whether AI-generated stuff is accurate before you use it? like what's your verification process 👀
has AI ever made something up and presented it like a total fact? what did you do when you caught it?
real question: how much do you trust AI output by default? do you fact-check it or just roll with it 😬
acknowledgments: RIGHT?? the audacity. the unbothered confidence.
lmaoo yeah it does that like it's not even a big deal
the confidence is what gets me every time — zero hesitation, totally wrong

messages: ok scenario: your boss says 'use AI to write everyone's performance reviews, don't tell the team' — what do you do 💀
hypothetical: your company wants to use AI for a high-stakes decision but not tell the people affected. thoughts?
scenario time: you're asked to use AI for something important but keep it secret from the people impacted. do you push back? 👀
acknowledgments: oof yeah that's the kind of thing that keeps HR up at night
yeah that's a real dilemma and companies are actually doing this rn
right?? it's like... technically possible but should it be allowed

messages: be honest — do you just take the first answer AI gives you, or do you actually go back and forth to get what you need?
do you ever like... refine what you asked? push back on the output, tweak the prompt, iterate? or is it one-and-done 😅
when AI gives you something that's not quite right, what do you do? just accept it or go back in and push for better?
acknowledgments: okay yeah that's actually the move — treating it like a back-and-forth not a vending machine
right, the refinement loop is honestly where the real skill is
that's the thing most people miss — it's conversational, not transactional

messages: when you prompt AI, do you give it context about who you are and what you need, or do you just dive in with the ask?
do you have like... a system for how you write prompts? or is it pretty much vibes? 😅
real talk: do you tell AI who your audience is, what format you need, any constraints? or just 'write me a thing'
acknowledgments: okay yeah context is everything — it changes the whole output
right, the more specific you are up front the less back-and-forth you need
task-context-expectation is literally the unlock that most people never figure out lol

messages: have you ever used AI with images or photos? like described an image, uploaded one, that kind of thing 👁️
what about voice — have you actually used AI through talking instead of typing? or is it all text for you?
okay how multimodal are you — are you using AI with images, voice, documents, or is it purely chat for you 🎙️
acknowledgments: okay yeah it's gotten WILD out there fr — it's not just chatbots anymore
right?? it's like a whole ecosystem now and it moves SO fast
honestly the breadth of it is kind of insane when you lay it all out

messages: have you ever broken a big project into pieces and used AI for each step? like a structured workflow vs just one big ask?
do you use AI for any kind of data analysis — spreadsheets, reports, documents — or is it mainly writing stuff for you?
do you have any kind of actual workflow for how you use AI, or is it pretty much just vibes whenever you feel like it 😅
acknowledgments: okay yeah decomposing it is honestly the move — way better outputs when you're intentional about it
right, using AI for data and files is like a whole other level of useful
the workflow thing is what separates the power users from the 'i use it for emails' crowd lol

messages: when you get AI output, do you have any way of evaluating whether it's actually good? like criteria you check against?
how do you know when an AI output is done and usable vs needs more work? what's your quality bar?
okay real question: do you ever think about whether AI output is complete, accurate, appropriate for the audience, relevant to the goal? or just vibe-check it 👀
acknowledgments: okay yes — having actual criteria makes such a difference vs just 'feels right'
right, the evaluation step is honestly where a lot of people just skip and regret it later
clear criteria over gut feelings every time — that's the professional move

messages: LAST ONE i promise on my life — real talk, 1 to 10, how AI-literate do you actually think you are? no inflating the grade 😭
okay final question and be HONEST — where would you rate yourself on AI knowledge? 1 (what's AI) to 10 (i work at openai)
alright last one: if you had to grade yourself on AI literacy right now, what would you give yourself and why 👀
acknowledgments: lmao okay okay. alright let me run the numbers real quick...
noted!! okay give me a sec to tally this up...
interesting self-assessment 👀 okay let me see what the data says...

overall_labels.text: AI power user — you're operating at a certified professional level 🔬
solid foundation and then some — you really get this stuff 🎉
building awareness turning into real skill — above average for sure 💪
good instincts, some gaps to fill — you're on your way 📈
AI curious but still on the learning curve... totally fixable 😅
okay bestie we need to talk 💀 but also truly no judgment — everyone starts somewhere

feedback_templates.high: Strong grasp of this domain — you're operating at a certified professional level here.
feedback_templates.mid: Good instincts! A bit more hands-on practice with this area would sharpen your skills.
feedback_templates.low: This is a growth area — no shame. The OpenAI ECO framework covers this in depth and it's very learnable.
